theme_and_archetype.archetype: Archetype 4 (Swiss & High-Contrast - Functional SaaS) blended with Archetype 1 (Organic & Trust)
theme_and_archetype.description: A clean, modern SaaS aesthetic utilizing slate greys for structure and soft teal/blue for primary actions. Prioritizes legibility, function-first layout, and crisp, polished UI elements with subtle ambient shadows.

rules.headings: Tight letter spacing (-0.02em to -0.03em). Use font-weight 600 or 700. Never center headings unless in a standalone card.
rules.body: Relaxed line-height (1.6) for readability. Crisp font-weight 400 or 500.
rules.labels: Overlines and table headers must be uppercase, tiny (0.7rem), tracking-wider (0.05em), and font-weight 600.

spacing_and_layout.sidebar: Light sidebar with 1px border-right. Active links should use a soft teal background (#F0FDFA) with primary text color (#0F766E). Inactive links are slate-600 hovering to slate-900 with #F1F5F9 background.

component_styles.buttons: Remove legacy gradients and heavy borders. Use solid backgrounds for primary buttons with subtle hover lift (translateY(-1px)) and smooth shadow. Border-radius: 8px. Ghost buttons should have transparent background, Slate-200 border, hovering to Slate-50 background.
component_styles.tables: Data tables need sticky headers with slate-50 background, uppercase slate-500 text. Rows should have a 1px slate-200 border-bottom, hovering to slate-50. Remove excessive zebra striping unless dataset is huge. Keep it airy.
component_styles.badges: Soft background tints (10% opacity of border color) with crisp matching text. Dot indicators inside badges for status context.
component_styles.cards: Flat white background, 1px slate-200 border, shadow-sm by default. Hover to shadow-md. No heavy outlines.
component_styles.login_page: High-end glassmorphism card (white/80, backdrop-blur-2xl, 1px white/40 border) over a bright, modern hotel atrium background. Clean inputs with slate borders focusing to teal.
component_styles.inputs: Height 40px, border Slate-200, background white. On focus: border Teal-600, ring 3px Teal-50. No thick shadows.

login_background.description: Spacious modern hotel atrium with sophisticated design and ambient lighting.

staff_avatar.description: Professional group portrait with diverse team standing confidently.

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. UPDATE :root VARIABLES in globals.css: Change all variables to match the new slate/teal color palette. Set --sidebar-bg to #FFFFFF. Set --bg to #F8FAFC. Replace heavy shadows with the new subtle, polished --shadow-sm, --shadow-md, etc.
2. UPDATE SIDEBAR STYLES: Since the sidebar is now light, modify `.sidebar-brand-name`, `.sidebar-brand-sub`, `.sidebar-section-label` to use slate text colors (--text-primary, --text-muted).

3. UPDATE SIDEBAR LINKS: Change `.sidebar-link` color to var(--text-secondary). On hover, change background to #F1F5F9 and color to var(--text-primary). For `.sidebar-link.active`, use background: var(--primary-light), color: var(--primary), and border-color: transparent (or soft teal).

4. UPDATE BUTTON STYLES: Strip linear-gradients from buttons (like `.btn-mod-cta`, `.btn-mod-dispatch`). Use solid var(--primary) or slate/teal combinations. Ensure clean hover states with box-shadow adjustments.
5. UPDATE TABLE STYLES: Ensure `thead th` uses background: #F8FAFC and color: var(--text-muted). Rows should be clean with subtle hover.

6. UPDATE LOGIN PAGE: Replace the dark background gradient/image with the new `login_background` URL from `media_urls`. Update `.login-glass-card` to be light-mode glass (background: rgba(255,255,255,0.85), backdrop-filter: blur(24px)). Fix login text colors to be slate-900.
7. REMOVE GOLD ACCENTS: Replace the legacy gold (#C5A880) with a modern accent color (like soft blue #3B82F6 or just use Teal). Modern SaaS doesn't use muddy gold unless requested.
8. ENSURE ACCESSIBILITY: Keep contrast ratios high (Teal-700 on white is great, Slate-600 on white is great).
9. TEST IDS: Do not forget to add/keep data-testid attributes if you touch HTML/React files, but since you are modifying CSS mainly, focus on the styling rules.
